content.full_text:
CITY OF FAIRFIELD
1000 WEBSTER STREET
FAIRFIELD, CA 94533
[707] 428-7419

Mar 5 8:28 AM '90

Incorporated December 12, 1903

March 1, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
Conflict of Interest Division
428 J Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Sir or Madam:

I am the City Attorney of the City of Fairfield and I am
writing this letter on behalf of Gary J. Falati, Mayor of the
City of Fairfield.

Mayor Falati is under contract to purchase a home to use as his
residence in a new subdivision in Fairfield. The subdivision
has been annexed to the City of Fairfield but surrounds on
three sides a piece of property which remains in the County of
Solano. The owner of the property which remains within the
County has proposed that the property be annexed to the City of
Fairfield. Pursuant to Government Code § 56000, et seq., the
Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) will consider the
annexation proposal and has the authority to approve, deny or
conditionally approve the proposal. Mayor Falati is a member
of the LAFCO Board. The home Mayor Falati is under contract to
purchase is located approximately 450 feet from the boundary of
the property proposed to be annexed. The purpose of this
letter is to request advice pursuant to Government Code § 83115
on the following question: May Mayor Falati vote or otherwise
participate in the LAFCO decision on the proposed annexation?

Thank you very much for your consideration and attention.

Sincerely,

Betsy Strauss
City Attorney


c: Gary Falati

sections.question: May Mayor Falati vote or otherwise participate in the LAFCO decision on the proposed annexation?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Mayor of Fairfield, who is under contract to purchase a home 450 feet from a property proposed for annexation to the city, may vote or participate in the Local Agency Formation Commission's decision on that annexation proposal.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or FPPC advice is provided in this document. The question is clearly stated near the end of the letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: May Mayor Falati vote or otherwise participate in the LAFCO decision on the proposed annexation?

Pursuant to Government Code § 56000, et seq., the Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) will consider the annexation proposal and has the authority to approve, deny or conditionally approve the proposal. Mayor Falati is a member of the LAFCO Board. The home Mayor Falati is under contract to purchase is located approximately 450 feet from the boundary of the property proposed to be annexed. The purpose of this letter is to request advice pursuant to Government Code § 83115 on the following question: May Mayor Falati vote or otherwise participate in the LAFCO decision on the proposed annexation? embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Fairfield requests FPPC advice on behalf of Mayor Gary Falati regarding his ability to vote on a LAFCO annexation proposal for property located approximately 450 feet from his contracted residence in a subdivision.
